args.prompt:
<system-reminder>

</system-reminder>

<Work_Context>

PLAN PATH: .sisyphus/plans/web3agent-framework.md (READ ONLY — NEVER MODIFY)
Always APPEND to notepad files — never overwrite.
</Work_Context>

## 1. TASK
Task 5 of 11: Implement the hosted Blockscout upstream adapter using MCP Client API.

Working directory: `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent`

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] `src/upstream/blockscout/adapter.ts` — full UpstreamAdapter implementation with transport fallback
- [ ] `tests/upstream/blockscout-adapter.test.ts` — mocked successful connection, prefix tests
- [ ] `tests/upstream/blockscout-degraded.test.ts` — unreachable endpoint → degraded startup
- [ ] Evidence: `.sisyphus/evidence/task-5-blockscout.txt` and `task-5-blockscout-error.txt`
- [ ] Git commit: `feat(blockscout): add hosted upstream adapter with transport fallback`

Verification:
```
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/blockscout-adapter.test.ts
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/blockscout-degraded.test.ts
pnpm run typecheck
```

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- Bash: run commands in `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent`
- Write: create new files
- Read: check existing interfaces before implementing

## 4. MUST DO

### Read frozen interfaces first
Read these files before writing any code:
- `src/types/upstream.ts` — UpstreamAdapter, AdapterHealth, PrefixedTool
- `src/types/health.ts` — BackendStatus, BackendStatusCode
- `src/types/tools.ts` — formatToolError

### src/upstream/blockscout/adapter.ts
EXACT import paths (required for MCP SDK 1.27.1):
```typescript
import { Client } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js";
import { StreamableHTTPClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/streamableHttp.js";
import { SSEClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/sse.js";
import type { UpstreamAdapter, AdapterHealth, PrefixedTool } from "../../types/upstream.js";
import type { BackendStatusCode } from "../../types/health.js";
```

Implementation requirements:
1. **Constructor**: Accept `blockscoutMcpUrl: string` (default: `"https://mcp.blockscout.com/mcp"`)
2. **initialize()**: 
   a. Try `StreamableHTTPClientTransport` first: `new StreamableHTTPClientTransport(new URL(url))`
   b. If that fails with connection/transport error, fall back to `SSEClientTransport`: `new SSEClientTransport(new URL(url))`
   c. If both fail → mark `status = "degraded"`, `fatalError = false`, return (do NOT throw)
   d. After connecting: call `client.callTool({ name: "__unlock_blockchain_analysis__", arguments: {} })` — this is the required bootstrap step
   e. Fetch tool list: `await client.listTools()` 
   f. Filter out `__unlock_blockchain_analysis__` from the list
   g. Prefix remaining tools: `blockscout_${originalName}`
   h. Build route map: `Map<string, string>` (prefixed name → original name)
   
3. **Expected tools** (15 after filtering): `get_chains_list`, `get_address_by_ens_name`, `lookup_token_by_symbol`, `get_contract_abi`, `inspect_contract_code`, `get_address_info`, `get_tokens_by_address`, `get_block_number`, `get_transactions_by_address`, `get_token_transfers_by_address`, `nft_tokens_by_address`, `get_block_info`, `get_transaction_info`, `read_contract`, `direct_api_call`

4. **getTools()**: Return array of `PrefixedTool` objects with prefixed names
5. **callTool(prefixedName, args)**: Strip prefix → forward to `client.callTool({ name: originalName, arguments: args })`
6. **getHealth()**: Return `AdapterHealth` with status, tool count, restart info
7. **shutdown()**: `await client.close()`

Complete class structure:
```typescript
export class BlockscoutAdapter implements UpstreamAdapter {
  public readonly name = "blockscout";
  private client: Client;
  private tools: PrefixedTool[] = [];
  private routeMap = new Map<string, string>(); // prefixed → original
  private health: AdapterHealth;
  private url: string;

  constructor(url: string = "https://mcp.blockscout.com/mcp") {
    this.url = url;
    this.client = new Client({ name: "web3agent", version: "0.1.0" });
    this.health = {
      name: "blockscout",
      status: "unavailable",
      message: "Not initialized",
    };
  }

  async initialize(): Promise<void> { ... }
  getTools(): PrefixedTool[] { return this.tools; }
  async callTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<unknown> { ... }
  getHealth(): AdapterHealth { return this.health; }
  async shutdown(): Promise<void> { try { await this.client.close(); } catch {} }
}
```

### tests/upstream/blockscout-adapter.test.ts
Use `vi.mock` to mock the MCP Client. Test:
1. Tools are prefixed with `blockscout_`
2. `__unlock_blockchain_analysis__` is NOT in the exposed tools
3. Route map maps prefixed → original names
4. callTool strips prefix and forwards correctly

```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from "vitest";
import { BlockscoutAdapter } from "../../src/upstream/blockscout/adapter.js";

// Mock the MCP SDK
vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js", () => ({
  Client: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    connect: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
    listTools: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({
      tools: [
        { name: "__unlock_blockchain_analysis__", description: "Bootstrap", inputSchema: { type: "object" } },
        { name: "get_address_info", description: "Get address info", inputSchema: { type: "object" } },
        { name: "get_block_number", description: "Get block number", inputSchema: { type: "object" } },
      ]
    }),
    callTool: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: [{ type: "text", text: "ok" }] }),
    close: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
  })),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/streamableHttp.js", () => ({
  StreamableHTTPClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({})),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/sse.js", () => ({
  SSEClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({})),
}));

describe("BlockscoutAdapter", () => {
  let adapter: BlockscoutAdapter;

  beforeEach(async () => {
    adapter = new BlockscoutAdapter("https://mock.blockscout.test/mcp");
    await adapter.initialize();
  });

  it("prefixes all tools with blockscout_", () => {
    const tools = adapter.getTools();
    expect(tools.every(t => t.name.startsWith("blockscout_"))).toBe(true);
  });

  it("filters out __unlock_blockchain_analysis__", () => {
    const tools = adapter.getTools();
    expect(tools.some(t => t.name.includes("unlock_blockchain_analysis"))).toBe(false);
  });

  it("exposes get_address_info as blockscout_get_address_info", () => {
    const tools = adapter.getTools();
    expect(tools.some(t => t.name === "blockscout_get_address_info")).toBe(true);
  });

  it("forwards callTool with stripped prefix", async () => {
    const result = await adapter.callTool("blockscout_get_address_info", { address: "0x1" });
    expect(result).toBeDefined();
  });

  it("health reports ok after successful init", () => {
    expect(adapter.getHealth().status).toBe("ok");
  });
});
```

### tests/upstream/blockscout-degraded.test.ts
Test that connection failure → degraded status, not crash:

```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
import { BlockscoutAdapter } from "../../src/upstream/blockscout/adapter.js";

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js", () => ({
  Client: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    connect: vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error("ECONNREFUSED")),
    listTools: vi.fn(),
    callTool: vi.fn(),
    close: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
  })),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/streamableHttp.js", () => ({
  StreamableHTTPClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({})),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/sse.js", () => ({
  SSEClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({})),
}));

describe("BlockscoutAdapter — degraded", () => {
  it("marks degraded when endpoint unreachable", async () => {
    const adapter = new BlockscoutAdapter("https://127.0.0.1:9/mcp");
    await adapter.initialize(); // must NOT throw
    expect(adapter.getHealth().status).toBe("degraded");
  });

  it("exposes zero tools when degraded", async () => {
    const adapter = new BlockscoutAdapter("https://127.0.0.1:9/mcp");
    await adapter.initialize();
    expect(adapter.getTools()).toHaveLength(0);
  });
});
```

### Save evidence and commit
```bash
mkdir -p .sisyphus/evidence
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/blockscout-adapter.test.ts 2>&1 > .sisyphus/evidence/task-5-blockscout.txt
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/blockscout-degraded.test.ts 2>&1 > .sisyphus/evidence/task-5-blockscout-error.txt
git add src/upstream/blockscout/ tests/upstream/blockscout*.test.ts
git commit -m "feat(blockscout): add hosted upstream adapter with transport fallback"
```

### Append to notepad
Append to `.sisyphus/notepads/web3agent-framework/learnings.md`:
```
## [2026-03-06] Task 5: Blockscout Adapter
- Import paths for MCP client transports (note the .js extension)
- bootstrap tool handling: callTool before listTools
- Any import path issues discovered
- Mock pattern for MCP Client in vitest
```

## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT expose `__unlock_blockchain_analysis__` in the proxy tool surface
- Do NOT throw/crash when Blockscout is unavailable — mark degraded and continue
- Do NOT modify upstream tool descriptions (proxy verbatim with prefix)
- Do NOT depend on Python subprocess
- Do NOT use npm — pnpm only
- Do NOT modify src/types/ files

## 6. CONTEXT

### Notepad
- READ: `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent/.sisyphus/notepads/web3agent-framework/learnings.md`
- WRITE: Append to learnings.md

### Inherited Wisdom
- Module resolution: `"nodenext"` — ALL src/ imports need `.js` extensions
- Installed: MCP SDK 1.27.1, viem 2.47.0, vitest 2.1.9, typescript 5.9.3, biome 1.9.4
- Biome: no console.log — use `process.stderr.write()`. Ignore list: dist/**, node_modules/**, .sisyphus/**, .argus/**
- Error pattern: `formatToolError(code, message, details?)` from `src/utils/errors.js`
- Frozen types: `src/types/upstream.ts` implements UpstreamAdapter interface
- Wave 2 complete: config/chains/wallet subsystems all operational

### SSEClientTransport import note
SSEClientTransport is deprecated in SDK 1.27.1 but still functional as fallback. Import it, use it only as a fallback after StreamableHTTPClientTransport fails.

args.prompt:
<system-reminder>

[SYSTEM DIRECTIVE: OH-MY-OPENCODE - SINGLE TASK ONLY]

**STOP. READ THIS BEFORE PROCEEDING.**

PLAN PATH: .sisyphus/plans/web3agent-framework.md (READ ONLY — NEVER MODIFY)
Always APPEND to notepad files — never overwrite.
</Work_Context>

## 1. TASK
Task 6 of 11: Implement the managed EVM MCP subprocess adapter with wallet-sync lifecycle.

Working directory: `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent`

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME
- [ ] `src/upstream/evm/adapter.ts` — full UpstreamAdapter with subprocess management, wallet-sync
- [ ] `tests/upstream/evm-adapter-readonly.test.ts` — startup without wallet, read-only mode
- [ ] `tests/upstream/evm-adapter-wallet-refresh.test.ts` — wallet-changed event triggers restart
- [ ] Evidence: `.sisyphus/evidence/task-6-evm.txt` and `task-6-evm-refresh.txt`
- [ ] Git commit: `feat(evm): add managed subprocess adapter with wallet sync`

Verification:
```
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/evm-adapter-readonly.test.ts
pnpm test -- tests/upstream/evm-adapter-wallet-refresh.test.ts
pnpm run typecheck
```

## 3. REQUIRED TOOLS
- Bash: run commands in `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent`
- Write: create new files
- Read: check existing interfaces

## 4. MUST DO

### Read existing code first
Read these before coding:
- `src/types/upstream.ts` — UpstreamAdapter, AdapterHealth, PrefixedTool
- `src/wallet/events.ts` — WalletEventEmitter, walletEvents
- `src/types/wallet.ts` — WalletState
- `src/types/health.ts` — BackendStatusCode

### src/upstream/evm/adapter.ts
Import paths:
```typescript
import { Client } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js";
import { StdioClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js";
import { walletEvents } from "../../wallet/events.js";
import type { WalletState } from "../../types/wallet.js";
import type { UpstreamAdapter, AdapterHealth, PrefixedTool } from "../../types/upstream.js";
```

Implementation requirements:

1. **Subprocess spawn**: Use `npx -y @mcpdotdirect/evm-mcp-server` command
   - Connect via `StdioClientTransport`: `new StdioClientTransport({ command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@mcpdotdirect/evm-mcp-server"], env: whitelistedEnv })`
   - Do NOT pass full parent env — explicitly whitelist these vars only:
     - `PATH`, `HOME`, `TMPDIR`, `TERM`, `NODE_ENV` (system essentials)
     - `EVM_PRIVATE_KEY` (from framework `PRIVATE_KEY`)
     - `EVM_MNEMONIC` (from framework `MNEMONIC`)
     - `EVM_ACCOUNT_INDEX` (from framework `WALLET_ACCOUNT_INDEX`)
     - `ETHERSCAN_API_KEY` (pass through as-is)

2. **Env var mapping**:
   ```typescript
   function buildEvmEnv(walletState?: WalletState): Record<string, string> {
     const env: Record<string, string> = {
       PATH: process.env.PATH ?? "",
       HOME: process.env.HOME ?? "",
       TMPDIR: process.env.TMPDIR ?? "/tmp",
     };
     if (process.env.PRIVATE_KEY) env.EVM_PRIVATE_KEY = process.env.PRIVATE_KEY;
     if (process.env.MNEMONIC) env.EVM_MNEMONIC = process.env.MNEMONIC;
     if (process.env.WALLET_ACCOUNT_INDEX) env.EVM_ACCOUNT_INDEX = process.env.WALLET_ACCOUNT_INDEX;
     if (process.env.ETHERSCAN_API_KEY) env.ETHERSCAN_API_KEY = process.env.ETHERSCAN_API_KEY;
     return env;
   }
   ```
   Note: After wallet activation, use the persisted wallet file's values, not just env vars. The walletState passed from the wallet-changed event has `mode` but not the actual credentials. Credentials come from env vars.

3. **Tool discovery**: `await client.listTools()` → prefix with `evm_` → build route map
   - Do NOT proxy prompts (EVM MCP has 10 prompts, but we skip them)

4. **Wallet-sync lifecycle**:
   ```typescript
   walletEvents.on("wallet-changed", async (state: WalletState) => {
     await this.restart();
   });
   ```
   `restart()` method: close client → kill subprocess → respawn with new env → re-fetch tools → update health

5. **Read-only mode**: Start without `EVM_PRIVATE_KEY`/`EVM_MNEMONIC`. EVM MCP still works for reads.

6. **Cleanup handlers**:
   ```typescript
   process.on("exit", () => this.killSubprocess());
   process.on("SIGTERM", () => this.killSubprocess());
   process.on("SIGINT", () => this.killSubprocess());
   ```
   `killSubprocess()`: synchronous, calls `subprocess.kill()`

7. **Health reporting**: Track `restartCount`, `pid`, `uptimeMs` (from start timestamp), `lastRestartAt`

8. **Degraded on failure**: If subprocess fails to start → mark degraded, expose 0 tools, do NOT crash

Full class:
```typescript
export class EvmAdapter implements UpstreamAdapter {
  public readonly name = "evm";
  private client!: Client;
  private transport!: StdioClientTransport;
  private tools: PrefixedTool[] = [];
  private routeMap = new Map<string, string>();
  private health: AdapterHealth;
  private startedAt?: Date;
  private restartCount = 0;
  
  constructor() {
    this.health = { name: "evm", status: "unavailable", message: "Not initialized" };
  }
  
  async initialize(): Promise<void> { ... }
  private async start(): Promise<void> { ... }
  private async restart(): Promise<void> { ... }
  private killSubprocess(): void { ... }
  getTools(): PrefixedTool[] { return this.tools; }
  async callTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<unknown> { ... }
  getHealth(): AdapterHealth { return this.health; }
  async shutdown(): Promise<void> { ... }
}
```

### tests/upstream/evm-adapter-readonly.test.ts
Mock the subprocess (don't actually spawn npx):
```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from "vitest";

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js", () => ({
  Client: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    connect: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
    listTools: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({
      tools: [
        { name: "get_balance", description: "Get balance", inputSchema: { type: "object" } },
        { name: "get_block_number", description: "Get block", inputSchema: { type: "object" } },
      ]
    }),
    callTool: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: [{ type: "text", text: "ok" }] }),
    close: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
  })),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js", () => ({
  StdioClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    process: { pid: 12345, kill: vi.fn() },
  })),
}));

describe("EvmAdapter — read-only", () => {
  it("starts cleanly without wallet credentials", async () => {
    const { EvmAdapter } = await import("../../src/upstream/evm/adapter.js");
    const adapter = new EvmAdapter();
    await adapter.initialize();
    expect(adapter.getHealth().status).toBe("ok");
  });

  it("prefixes tools with evm_", async () => {
    const { EvmAdapter } = await import("../../src/upstream/evm/adapter.js");
    const adapter = new EvmAdapter();
    await adapter.initialize();
    const tools = adapter.getTools();
    expect(tools.every(t => t.name.startsWith("evm_"))).toBe(true);
  });

  it("degrades gracefully when subprocess fails", async () => {
    // Override connect mock to fail
    vi.mocked(Client).mockImplementationOnce(() => ({
      connect: vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error("spawn ENOENT")),
      listTools: vi.fn(),
      callTool: vi.fn(),
      close: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
    }) as any);
    const { EvmAdapter } = await import("../../src/upstream/evm/adapter.js");
    const adapter = new EvmAdapter();
    await adapter.initialize();
    expect(adapter.getHealth().status).toBe("degraded");
    expect(adapter.getTools()).toHaveLength(0);
  });
});
```

### tests/upstream/evm-adapter-wallet-refresh.test.ts
```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from "vitest";
import { walletEvents } from "../../src/wallet/events.js";
import type { WalletState } from "../../src/types/wallet.js";

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js", () => ({
  Client: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    connect: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
    listTools: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ tools: [{ name: "get_balance", description: "", inputSchema: { type: "object" } }] }),
    callTool: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: [] }),
    close: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),
  })),
}));

vi.mock("@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js", () => ({
  StdioClientTransport: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => ({
    process: { pid: 99, kill: vi.fn() },
  })),
}));

describe("EvmAdapter — wallet refresh", () => {
  it("restart increments restartCount after wallet-changed", async () => {
    const { EvmAdapter } = await import("../../src/upstream/evm/adapter.js");
    const adapter = new EvmAdapter();
    await adapter.initialize();
    
    const initialCount = adapter.getHealth().restartCount ?? 0;
    
    // Emit wallet-changed
    walletEvents.emit("wallet-changed", {
      mode: "private-key",
      address: "0x1",
      chainId: 8453,
      accountIndex: 0,
      addressIndex: 0,
    } as WalletState);
    
    // Give async restart time to run
    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50));
    
    const health = adapter.getHealth();
    expect((health.restartCount ?? 0)).toBeGreaterThan(initialCount);
  });
});
```


## 5. MUST NOT DO
- Do NOT pass full parent environment to subprocess — whitelist only
- Do NOT log secrets (private keys, mnemonics) to stderr
- Do NOT proxy EVM MCP prompts — tools only
- Do NOT treat restart as fire-and-forget — must complete before serving new calls
- Do NOT crash startup if EVM MCP is unavailable — degrade gracefully
- Do NOT use npm — pnpm only

## 6. CONTEXT

### Notepad
- READ: `/Users/ignacioblitzer/Develop/defizoo/web3agent/.sisyphus/notepads/web3agent-framework/learnings.md`
- WRITE: Append to learnings.md

### Inherited Wisdom
- Module resolution: `"nodenext"` — ALL src/ imports need `.js` extensions
- Installed: MCP SDK 1.27.1, viem 2.47.0, vitest 2.1.9, typescript 5.9.3, biome 1.9.4
- `src/wallet/events.ts` exists: `walletEvents` EventEmitter with `wallet-changed` event
- `src/types/upstream.ts` exists: UpstreamAdapter interface to implement
- `src/types/wallet.ts` exists: WalletState type
- Biome: no console.log — use `process.stderr.write()`
- Wave 2 complete: wallet subsystem operational, confirmation queue operational

### Critical: EVM MCP Package
- Package: `@mcpdotdirect/evm-mcp-server` v2.0.4
- Invoked via: `npx -y @mcpdotdirect/evm-mcp-server`
- Env vars it uses: `EVM_PRIVATE_KEY`, `EVM_MNEMONIC`, `EVM_ACCOUNT_INDEX`, `ETHERSCAN_API_KEY`
- Has 25 tools and 10 prompts — proxy tools only, skip prompts
- Works in read-only mode without wallet credentials
